title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching · Type-System Trace: Bounded Batch Window
summary: A publisher awaits a boolean from publish, ignores false, buffers unbounded work during broker flow control, and batches until memory exhaustion while upstream requests continue to receive success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bounded batch window increment in publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Batch by count, bytes, age, confirm window, and cancellation while retaining each message result and fairness.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Bounded Batch Window · Theory Bounded Batch Window
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7004 in a regional incident workflow during one broker-node restart. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Bounded Batch Window contract for the supplied data.
